items.summary: Update the backend stable owner principal variable to 'w3w26-hrxnk-kfpaw-7trqf-ngcqt-xm3i3-qebzp-vtech-eqcjs-nahkw-fae'. Ensure all hasPermission checks for #owner role use this principal. The authorized user (Ryan) retains #authorized role with CRUD access to clients, jobs, inventory, and other standard operations, but cannot change labor rates or view owner-only metrics.

Add a 'Labor Rates' quick-access card or section on the Dashboard page that is only visible to the #owner role. The card should display a summary of existing labor rates (name, type, amount) and include a button that navigates to the Labor Rates management section in Settings. If the caller is not #owner, this section must not render.

Add an 'Inventory' quick-access card or section on the Dashboard page visible to both #owner and #authorized roles. The card should display a summary count of total parts, number of low-stock items, and a button that navigates to the full Inventory screen. Low-stock count should be derived from listParts results where quantity <= lowStockThreshold.

Ensure the Dashboard metrics section (open jobs count, in-progress jobs count, complete jobs count, total clients count, and any revenue or summary statistics) is only visible to the #owner role. Authorized users navigating to the Dashboard should see jobs and inventory sections but not the owner-only metrics cards.
